questions.question: What is the brain's waste-clearance system that operates primarily during deep sleep?
questions.explanation: The glymphatic system clears toxic waste products from the brain during deep sleep, functioning like a nightly cleaning crew for your neural pathways.

questions.question: Which chemical builds up in the brain during waking hours to create 'sleep pressure'?
questions.explanation: Adenosine gradually accumulates in your brain throughout the day, creating increasing sleep pressure. This is part of Process S (homeostatic sleep drive).

questions.question: What are the two biological processes that regulate sleep according to sleep science?

options.text: Process S (sleep drive) and Process C (circadian rhythm)

questions.explanation: Process S (homeostatic sleep drive) builds sleep pressure through adenosine accumulation, while Process C (circadian rhythm) is your internal 24-hour clock that times when you feel alert and sleepy.

questions.question: During which sleep stage does the brain consolidate emotional memories and process difficult experiences?
questions.explanation: REM sleep is when your brain consolidates emotional memories and processes difficult experiences. It serves as your nightly therapist, helping you integrate and make sense of emotional events.

questions.question: What hormone does the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) signal the brain to produce as darkness falls?
questions.explanation: The suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN), your master circadian clock, signals the pineal gland to produce melatonin as darkness falls, preparing your body for sleep.

title: The Neuroscience of Sleep and Mental Health - Quiz
